title: Imagine
why_this_song: The imperative 'Imagine' repeats six times — an extended drill on a single verb form. Abstract nouns (heaven, hell, peace) give a vocabulary stretch.

vocab_hooks.note: verb — imperative form starts every verse

grammar_callouts: it's easy if you try — zero conditional
above / below as prepositions of place

exercises.prompt_en: Fill the blank in the opening line:
exercises.prompt_pt: Complete a lacuna do verso inicial:

exercises.excerpt: Imagine all the people living life in peace
exercises.model_answer_pt: Imagine todas as pessoas vivendo em paz
exercises.teacher_note: Notice 'living life' — gerund as noun modifier. In PT we drop 'life' in a natural translation.

exercises.prompt_en: Lennon asks you to imagine a world without religion or countries. Can you do that? What would change in your daily life?
exercises.prompt_pt: Lennon pede para imaginar um mundo sem religião ou países. Você consegue? O que mudaria na sua vida diária?

teaching_notes_md:
**Pedagogy:** Every verse opens with the imperative 'Imagine' — six reps in one song. Great for drilling. Abstract nouns push learners slightly beyond A1 comfort zone.

**Watch out:** 'Brotherhood of man' is dated language; discuss with students what more inclusive alternatives exist today.
